2606.30345.title: DRIFT: Difficulty Routing Self-DIstillation with Rhythm-Gated Exploration and Success BuFfer Training
2606.30345.paper_summary: Enabling large language models to achieve stable self-improvement without external expert supervision remains a central challenge in complex reasoning tasks. Existing self-distillation and reinforcement learning methods lack explicit mechanisms for tracking problem-level learning progress and adapting optimization strategies accordingly. Consequently, training may over-optimize easy problems, receive weak supervision from hard problems, and fail to sufficiently explore borderline cases. To resolve these issues, we propose DRIFT, an online self-evolution policy optimization framework for large language models. DRIFT regulates the model's self-improvement process through the joint use of Difficulty Routing and Rhythm Gating. The former identifies the model's learning state at the problem level and dynamically allocates self-distillation and reinforcement learning signals, while the latter refines policy updates at the token level, concentrating exploration on critical reasoning positions. By further incorporating a success buffer and a two-stage curriculum learning strategy, DRIFT preserves high-quality historical experience while progressively guiding the model from reliable behavior acquisition toward stable policy evolution. Evaluated across five benchmarks and three model scales, DRIFT surpasses the peak performance of both GRPO and SDPO across all evaluated metrics. On the average score over the five benchmarks, DRIFT achieves 79.5$\%$, outperforming GRPO by 9.5$\%$ and SDPO by 7.5$\%$, establishing a new state-of-the-art result. Notably, on ToolUse, DRIFT reaches an accuracy of 79.2$\%$, improving over GRPO by 13.5$\%$ and SDPO by 10.7$\%$, setting a new state-of-the-art and substantially outperforming all concurrent methods.
2606.30345.paper_summary_zh: 啟用大型語言模型在沒有外部專家監督的情況下實現穩定的自我改進，仍然是複雜推理任務中的一個核心挑戰。現有的自我蒸餾和強化學習方法缺乏明確的機制來跟踪問題層級的學習進展並相應地調整優化策略。因此，訓練可能會過度優化簡單問題，從困難問題中獲得薄弱的監督，並未能充分探索邊界案例。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了DRIFT，一個針對大型語言模型的在線自我演化政策優化框架。DRIFT通過難度路由和節奏閘的聯合使用來調節模型的自我改進過程。前者在問題層級識別模型的學習狀態並動態分配自我蒸餾和強化學習信號，而後者則在標記層級精煉政策更新，將探索集中在關鍵推理位置。通過進一步結合成功緩衝區和兩階段課程學習策略，DRIFT保留了高質量的歷史經驗，同時逐步引導模型從可靠的行為獲取走向穩定的政策演變。在五個基準和三個模型規模的評估中，DRIFT在所有評估指標上超越了GRPO和SDPO的峰值表現。在五個基準的平均得分上，DRIFT達到79.5$\%$，比GRPO高出9.5$\%$，比SDPO高出7.5$\%$，創造了新的最先進結果。值得注意的是，在ToolUse上，DRIFT達到79.2$\%$的準確率，比GRPO提高了13.5$\%$，比SDPO提高了10.7$\%$，創造了新的最先進結果，並顯著超越了所有當前方法。
2606.30345.author: Haisen Luo et.al.
2606.30345.authors: Haisen Luo, Yiwei Liu, Haoning Wang, Dan Liu, Junxi Yin, Haotian Wang, Lei Zhang, Xiaoyu Tian, Shuaiting Chen, Yuansheng Song, Baoyan Guo, Xiongfei Yan, Bolan Yang, Chengwei Liu, Ming Cui, Jiong Chen
